reasoning: Task appears to require a concise summary of the provided article about the Sir Patrick Leigh Fermor archive and its significance.
output: The National Library of Scotland has catalogued the archive of travel writer and war hero Sir Patrick Leigh Fermor, revealing thousands of items—including letters from Prince Charles and Truman Capote, literary manuscripts, sketches, and even an unpublished John Betjeman poem. The collection features the only surviving notebook from Fermor’s 1933 European trek, which inspired his famed books A Time of Gifts, Between the Woods and the Water, and The Broken Road. Fermor, who died in 2011 at 96, spent much of World War II in Nazi-occupied Crete where he led guerrilla operations; his exploits inspired the 1957 film Ill Met by Moonlight. The archive spans about 16 metres of shelving and will be digitised and possibly exhibited. It was donated by the John R Murray Charitable Trust, Fermor’s publisher, with cataloguing support funded by the trust.
